lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਖਿਓ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਸਰਬ ਮੰਗਲ ਰੂਪ ਉਲਟੀ ਸੰਤ ਕਰਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: dekhio drisatt sarab mangal roop ulattee sant karaae |
lines.english: With my eyes, I see the Lord, the Embodiment of Absolute Bliss; the Saint has turned away from the world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਰਣੀ ਚਲਉ ਮਾਰਗਿ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਕੈ ਰਸਨਾ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਏ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: charanee chlau maarag tthaakur kai rasanaa har gun gaae |2|
lines.english: With my feet, I walk on my Lord and Mater's Path. With my tongue, I sing the Glorious Praises of the Lord. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਵਨ ਉਪਮਾ ਕਉਨ ਬਡਾਈ ਕਿਆ ਗੁਨ ਕਹਉ ਰੀਝਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: kavan upamaa kaun baddaaee kiaa gun khau reejhaae |
lines.english: What praise, what glory and what virtues should I utter, in order to please the Lord?

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਇਓ ਲਾਲੁ ਅਮੋਲੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਹਰਿ ਛੋਡਿ ਨ ਕਤਹੂ ਜਾਏ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: paaeo laal amol naam har chhodd na katahoo jaae |3|
lines.english: I have found the Priceless Name of the Beloved Lord; it never leaves me or goes anywhere else. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੋਤ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾਲ ਦੀਨ ਦਇਆ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਦਾਸ ਦਸਾਏ ॥੪॥੮॥
lines.translit: hot kripaal deen deaa prabh jan naanak daas dasaae |4|8|
lines.english: That humble being, unto whom the Merciful Lord is kind - O servant Nanak, he is the slave of God's slaves. ||4||8||

lines.gurmukhi: ਓੁਇ ਸੁਖ ਕਾ ਸਿਉ ਬਰਨਿ ਸੁਨਾਵਤ ॥
lines.translit: oue sukh kaa siau baran sunaavat |
lines.english: Who can I tell, and with whom can I speak, about this state of peace and bliss?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਰਗ ਮਹਲਾ ੫ ॥
lines.translit: saarag mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Saarang, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਦ ਬਿਨੋਦ ਪੇਖਿ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਦਰਸਨ ਮਨਿ ਮੰਗਲ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਵਤ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: anad binod pekh prabh darasan man mangal gun gaavat |1| rahaau |
lines.english: I am in ecstasy and delight, gazing upon the Blessed Vision of God's Darshan. My mind sings His Songs of Joy and His Glories. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਸਮ ਭਈ ਪੇਖਿ ਬਿਸਮਾਦੀ ਪੂਰਿ ਰਹੇ ਕਿਰਪਾਵਤ ॥
lines.translit: bisam bhee pekh bisamaadee poor rahe kirapaavat |
lines.english: I am wonderstruck, gazing upon the Wondrous Lord. The Merciful Lord is All-pervading everywhere.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੀਓ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਾਮੁ ਅਮੋਲਕ ਜਿਉ ਚਾਖਿ ਗੂੰਗਾ ਮੁਸਕਾਵਤ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: peeo amrit naam amolak jiau chaakh goongaa musakaavat |1|
lines.english: I drink in the Invaluable Nectar of the Naam, the Name of the Lord. Like the mute, I can only smile - I cannot speak of its flavor. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੈਸੇ ਪਵਨੁ ਬੰਧ ਕਰਿ ਰਾਖਿਓ ਬੂਝ ਨ ਆਵਤ ਜਾਵਤ ॥
lines.translit: jaise pavan bandh kar raakhio boojh na aavat jaavat |
lines.english: As the breath is held in bondage, no one can understand its coming in and going out.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾ ਕਉ ਰਿਦੈ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸੁ ਭਇਓ ਹਰਿ ਉਆ ਕੀ ਕਹੀ ਨ ਜਾਇ ਕਹਾਵਤ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jaa kau ridai pragaas bheo har uaa kee kahee na jaae kahaavat |2|
lines.english: So is that person, whose heart is enlightened by the Lord - his story cannot be told. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਨ ਉਪਾਵ ਜੇਤੇ ਕਿਛੁ ਕਹੀਅਹਿ ਤੇਤੇ ਸੀਖੇ ਪਾਵਤ ॥
lines.translit: aan upaav jete kichh kaheeeh tete seekhe paavat |
lines.english: As many other efforts as you can think of - I have seen them and studied them all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਚਿੰਤ ਲਾਲੁ ਗ੍ਰਿਹ ਭੀਤਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਿਓ ਅਗਮ ਜੈਸੇ ਪਰਖਾਵਤ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: achint laal grih bheetar pragattio agam jaise parakhaavat |3|
lines.english: My Beloved, Carefree Lord has revealed Himself within the home of my own heart; thus I have realized the Inaccessible Lord. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਰਗੁਣ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰ ਅਬਿਨਾਸੀ ਅਤੁਲੋ ਤੁਲਿਓ ਨ ਜਾਵਤ ॥
lines.translit: niragun nirankaar abinaasee atulo tulio na jaavat |
lines.english: The Absolute, Formless, Eternally Unchanging, Immeasurable Lord cannot be measured.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਅਜਰੁ ਜਿਨਿ ਜਰਿਆ ਤਿਸ ਹੀ ਕਉ ਬਨਿ ਆਵਤ ॥੪॥੯॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak ajar jin jariaa tis hee kau ban aavat |4|9|
lines.english: Says Nanak, whoever endures the unendurable - this state belongs to him alone. ||4||9||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਖਈ ਦਿਨੁ ਰੈਨਿ ਇਵ ਹੀ ਗੁਦਾਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: bikhee din rain iv hee gudaarai |
lines.english: The corrupt person passes his days and nights uselessly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੋਬਿੰਦੁ ਨ ਭਜੈ ਅਹੰਬੁਧਿ ਮਾਤਾ ਜਨਮੁ ਜੂਐ ਜਿਉ ਹਾਰੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: gobind na bhajai ahanbudh maataa janam jooai jiau haarai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: He does not vibrate and meditate on the Lord of the Universe; he is intoxicated with egotistical intellect. He loses his life in the gamble. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਰਗ ਮਹਲਾ ੫ ॥
lines.translit: saarag mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Saarang, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੁ ਅਮੋਲਾ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਨ ਤਿਸ ਸਿਉ ਪਰ ਨਿੰਦਾ ਹਿਤਕਾਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: naam amolaa preet na tis siau par nindaa hitakaarai |
lines.english: The Naam, the Name of the Lord, is priceless, but he is not in love with it. He loves only to slander others.

lines.gurmukhi: ਛਾਪਰੁ ਬਾਂਧਿ ਸਵਾਰੈ ਤ੍ਰਿਣ ਕੋ ਦੁਆਰੈ ਪਾਵਕੁ ਜਾਰੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: chhaapar baandh savaarai trin ko duaarai paavak jaarai |1|
lines.english: Weaving the grass, he builds his house of straw. At the door, he builds a fire. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਢੈ ਬਸਤ੍ਰ ਕਾਜਰ ਮਹਿ ਪਰਿਆ ਬਹੁਰਿ ਬਹੁਰਿ ਫਿਰਿ ਝਾਰੈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: odtai basatr kaajar meh pariaa bahur bahur fir jhaarai |2|
lines.english: Wearing his good clothes, the mortal falls into the coal-pit; again and again, he tries to shake it off. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਲਰ ਪੋਟ ਉਠਾਵੈ ਮੂੰਡਹਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਮਨ ਤੇ ਡਾਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: kaalar pott utthaavai moonddeh amrit man te ddaarai |
lines.english: He carries a load of sulfur on his head, and drives the Ambrosial Nectar out of his mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਟੈ ਪੇਡੁ ਡਾਲ ਪਰਿ ਠਾਢੌ ਖਾਇ ਖਾਇ ਮੁਸਕਾਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: kaattai pedd ddaal par tthaadtau khaae khaae musakaarai |
lines.english: Standing on the branch, eating and eating and smiling, he cuts down the tree.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਿਰਿਓ ਜਾਇ ਰਸਾਤਲਿ ਪਰਿਓ ਛਿਟੀ ਛਿਟੀ ਸਿਰ ਭਾਰੈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: girio jaae rasaatal pario chhittee chhittee sir bhaarai |3|
lines.english: He falls down head-first and is shattered into bits and pieces. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਿਰਵੈਰੈ ਸੰਗਿ ਵੈਰੁ ਰਚਾਏ ਪਹੁਚਿ ਨ ਸਕੈ ਗਵਾਰੈ ॥
lines.translit: niravairai sang vair rachaae pahuch na sakai gavaarai |
lines.english: He bears vengeance against the Lord who is free of vengeance. The fool is not up to the task.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਸੰਤਨ ਕਾ ਰਾਖਾ ਪਾਰਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰੈ ॥੪॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak santan kaa raakhaa paarabraham nirankaarai |4|10|
lines.english: Says Nanak, the Saving Grace of the Saints is the Formless, Supreme Lord God. ||4||10||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਵਰਿ ਸਭਿ ਭੂਲੇ ਭ੍ਰਮਤ ਨ ਜਾਨਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: avar sabh bhoole bhramat na jaaniaa |
lines.english: All the others are deluded by doubt; they do not understand.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਰਗ ਮਹਲਾ ੫ ॥
lines.translit: saarag mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Saarang, Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕੁ ਸੁਧਾਖਰੁ ਜਾ ਕੈ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਵਸਿਆ ਤਿਨਿ ਬੇਦਹਿ ਤਤੁ ਪਛਾਨਿਆ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: ek sudhaakhar jaa kai hiradai vasiaa tin bedeh tat pachhaaniaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: That person, within whose heart the One Pure Word abides, realizes the essence of the Vedas. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਉ ਲਉ ਰਿਦੈ ਨਹੀ ਪਰਗਾਸਾ ਤਉ ਲਉ ਅੰਧ ਅੰਧਾਰਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jau lau ridai nahee paragaasaa tau lau andh andhaaraa |1|
lines.english: But as long as his heart is not enlightened, he is stuck in pitch black darkness. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਵਿਰਤਿ ਮਾਰਗੁ ਜੇਤਾ ਕਿਛੁ ਹੋਈਐ ਤੇਤਾ ਲੋਗ ਪਚਾਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: paravirat maarag jetaa kichh hoeeai tetaa log pachaaraa |
lines.english: He walks in the ways of the world, trying to please people.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਬਿਨੁ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨ ਹੋਈ ਹੈ ਤੁਟੈ ਨਾਹੀ ਅਭਿਮਾਨੈ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: raam naam bin mukat na hoee hai tuttai naahee abhimaanai |2|
lines.english: Just so, without the Lord's Name, no one is liberated, nor is egotistical pride eradicated. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੈਸੇ ਧਰਤੀ ਸਾਧੈ ਬਹੁ ਬਿਧਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਬੀਜੈ ਨਹੀ ਜਾਂਮੈ ॥
lines.translit: jaise dharatee saadhai bahu bidh bin beejai nahee jaamai |
lines.english: The land may be prepared in every way, but nothing sprouts without being planted.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਭੇਟੇ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨ ਕਾਹੂ ਮਿਲਤ ਨਹੀ ਜਗਦੀਸੈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: bin gur bhette mukat na kaahoo milat nahee jagadeesai |3|
lines.english: Without meeting the Guru, no one is liberated, and the Lord of the Universe is not met. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨੀਰੁ ਬਿਲੋਵੈ ਅਤਿ ਸ੍ਰਮੁ ਪਾਵੈ ਨੈਨੂ ਕੈਸੇ ਰੀਸੈ ॥
lines.translit: neer bilovai at sram paavai nainoo kaise reesai |
lines.english: The mortal may churn water until he is sore, but how can butter be produced?
